title: Audrey Lillie
text:
Recent graduate from the University of Oregon (2026). BS: Marine Biology, BS: Spatial Data Science, Clark Honors College, Sutherland Lab.
My interests include:

title: Demographic Change in Detroit, MI (Wayne County)
text: These are several analyses that I produced in the course “Spatial Demographics” taught by Billy Southern at the University of Oregon, Spring 2026. These analyses integrate information related to Census data (both ACS and 10-year).

title: Audrey Lillie
text:
Research Interests
Currently, I work under Dr. Kelly Sutherland in her lab “Form, Function, and Flow in the Plankton,” researching the zooplanktonic phylum Chaetognatha and their reproductive and morphological distribution in the Northern California Current.

title: Audrey Lillie
text:
About
I graduated from the University of Oregon in Spring 2026 with two Bachelors of Science degrees in Marine Biology and Spatial Data Science, and a certificate from the Clark Honors College.
Currently, I work under Dr. Kelly Sutherland in her lab “Form, Function, and Flow in the Plankton,” researching the zooplanktonic phylum Chaetognatha and their reproductive and morphological distribution in the Northern California Current.

text:
Visualizing Segregation and Gentrification

Multi-group Segregation Index











Dissimilarity Index:


NAMEBWDAWDHWDAnn Arbor0.4130.3320.234Detroit0.5880.7140.660Grand Rapids0.4790.3930.532Lansing0.3750.4960.304


Dissimilarity Indices are widely used demographic metrics that measure the evennes with which two distinct groups are distributed across a geography. It ranges from 0 to 1, where a 0 = complete integration and 1 = complete segregation.


Interaction Index (P*)


BWIAWIHWI1.6281.9671.741


The interaction index is a metric used to measure the probability of contact or exposure between two distinct groups (such as demographic populations) across residential or social zones.

title: Demographic Change in Detroit, MI (Wayne County)

text:
Location Quotient









The location quotient is a statistical ratio used in economic geography to measure how concentrated an industry, occupation, or demographic is in a specific region compared to a broader benchmark (i.e., the national average). If LQ = 1.0, the region is equally concentrated in that industry compared to the nation; it is economically diverse. If LQ > 1.0, the industry is more concentrated in the region than in the nation. If LQ < 1.0, the industry is less concentrated locally than at the national level
